question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user POST an arbitrarily large protobuf-encoded byte array to POST /api/v1/network/fees with no visible body-size enforcement in the controller, causing the server to allocate unbounded heap memory via Bytes.wrap(body) and crash the JVM (OOM/DoS)?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a protobuf payload with deeply nested or recursively-structured fields that causes Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(Bytes.wrap(body)) to consume excessive CPU during parsing, resulting in a CPU-exhaustion DoS against the fee estimation endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction containing thousands of artificially inflated SignaturePair entries in the sigMap field, causing numTxnSignatures() to return an arbitrarily large value and the fee calculator to produce a grossly incorrect (inflated or overflowed) fee estimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction whose serialized size as measured by Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord(transaction) in numTxnBytes() is maximized by padding fields with junk data, causing the byte-based fee components to return an astronomically inflated fee estimate that misleads clients?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with signedTransactionBytes set to a valid outer wrapper but with a bodyBytes field inside SignedTransaction that encodes a different transaction type than what the outer memo implies, causing the fee calculator to compute fees for the wrong HederaFunctionality and return a misleading estimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user send a transaction using the legacy bodyBytes path (transaction.bodyBytes().length() > 0) instead of signedTransactionBytes to bypass any signature-count-based fee components, obtaining a lower fee estimate than the actual network would charge?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where both signedTransactionBytes and bodyBytes are populated, exploiting the priority logic in TransactionFeeContext (signedTransactionBytes checked first) to have the fee calculated against a different body than what bodyBytes contains, producing an inconsistent fee estimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with mode=STATE and a topicID referencing a topic with expensive custom fees in the DB, causing FeeEstimationFeeContext.readableStore(ReadableTopicStore.class) to trigger a live DB read and return a STATE-mode fee estimate that is orders of magnitude higher than INTRINSIC, misleading the user about actual costs?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction in STATE mode referencing thousands of different topicIDs in rapid succession, causing repeated live DB reads via FeeTopicStore and exhausting DB connection pool resources (DoS)?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoTransfer transaction in STATE mode with a tokenID referencing a token with custom fees, causing FeeTokenStore to perform a live DB lookup and return a fee estimate inflated by 10,000,000 tinycents compared to INTRINSIC mode, misleading the user about the true cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with mode=STATE and a tokenID that does not exist in the DB, causing FeeTokenStore to return null and the fee calculator to silently skip custom-fee components, producing an underestimated fee that causes the user to underpay on the real network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an UnsupportedOperationException (HTTP 500) by submitting a transaction type in STATE mode that internally calls readableStore() with a store class not handled by FeeEstimationFeeContext (e.g., a store beyond ReadableTopicStore, ReadableTokenStore, ReadableAccountStore, etc.), leaking internal stack traces?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction body with an unknown/unsupported HederaFunctionality (no data case set) that causes functionOf(body) to throw UnknownHederaFunctionality, which is wrapped in IllegalStateException and re-thrown, potentially leaking exception details in the HTTP 400 response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCall transaction to the fee estimator and receive a total fee of 0 tinycents (as ContractCall fees are paid in gas and the calculator clears all hbar fees), using this to mislead other users into believing ContractCall transactions are free on the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user set highVolumeThrottle=10000 (maximum allowed by @Max(10000)) combined with a transaction body where highVolume=true and mode=STATE, causing the fee multiplier to reach the maximum configured customMaxRaw value and return an inflated fee estimate that could be used to manipulate client-side fee decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with highVolume=false in the body but highVolumeThrottle=10000 as a query parameter, confirming that the throttle parameter is silently ignored when highVolume is not set, and use this to obtain a fee estimate that does not reflect actual high-volume pricing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes feeResult.getHighVolumeMultiplier() to return a value that, when divided by HighVolumePricingCalculator.HIGH_VOLUME_MULTIPLIER_SCALE, produces an integer division result of 0 (truncation), causing the response to report a highVolumeMultiplier of 0 instead of the correct fractional value, misleading fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toExtra()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes detail.perUnit() * detail.charged() in toExtra() to overflow a long value, producing a negative or wrapped subtotal in the FeeExtra response, misleading clients about the true fee breakdown?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a memo field containing the maximum allowed bytes (100 bytes per HAPI spec) to maximize the numTxnBytes() value returned by Transaction.PROTOBUF.measureRecord(transaction), inflating the byte-based fee components in the estimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a message payload of maximum size (e.g., 2000+ bytes) to trigger the PROCESSING_BYTES extra fee component, causing the total fee estimate to jump from ~1,890,400 to ~96,320,000 tinycents, and use this to mislead clients about the cost of large messages?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an invalid/random signedTransactionBytes payload (e.g., 100 random bytes) that passes the outer Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse() but fails during SignedTransaction.PROTOBUF.parse() inside TransactionFeeContext, causing an IllegalArgumentException('Unable to parse transaction') HTTP 400 that leaks the parse failure path?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the POST /api/v1/network/fees endpoint with high-frequency requests containing valid but computationally expensive transactions (e.g., STATE mode with DB lookups), exhausting thread pool and DB connection resources and causing a denial-of-service for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: estimateFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a protobuf payload that contains unknown field tags (fields not defined in the Transaction schema) that are silently ignored by Transaction.PROTOBUF.parse(), allowing the attacker to embed arbitrary data in the request without triggering a parse error?
